topics.title: Neural Signatures of Chronic Pain Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on pain disorders and their neural correlates, especially fibromyalgia, trigeminal neuralgia, and chronic migraine. It also includes work on pain perception, central sensitization, and brain-based phenotypes or signatures of chronic pain.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome of temporal lobe epilepsy
topics.title: Epilepsy Network Dynamics and Surgical Targets
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy, especially temporal lobe and focal epilepsy, with emphasis on identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset zones in pediatric and drug-resistant cases. It also highlights connectome-based and dynamic functional connectivity approaches used to characterize epileptic brain dynamics and predict surgical outcome.

keywords: intrinsic striatal functional brain connectivity signature of compulsivity
topics.title: OCD Circuits and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, related symptoms, and comorbid anxiety, with emphasis on frontostriatal and CSTC circuit alterations. It also includes treatment-related findings on CBT and deep TMS outcomes.

topics.title: Noninvasive Brain Stimulation and Network Effects
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive brain stimulation approaches and their effects on cortical excitability, connectivity, and behavior. It also includes studies of stimulation parameters and neurophysiological measures across EEG, fMRI, and related outcomes.

keywords: lsd reshapes cortical dynamics through peak frequency shifts
topics.title: Psychedelic Effects on Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psilocybin, ketamine, and LSD alter cortical and large-scale brain dynamics, connectivity, and entropy. It also includes modeling and EEG/functional connectivity approaches used to quantify these drug-induced neural changes.

topics.title: Addiction Craving and Neural Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on substance use disorders, especially alcohol and opioid-related addiction, craving, and cue reactivity. It also highlights adolescent brain development, inhibitory control, reward, and functional connectivity as neural correlates of addiction.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Abnormalities in Autism
topics.description: This cluster focuses on developmental differences in autism and autism spectrum disorder, especially during childhood and youth. It highlights atypical brain organization, connectivity, and behavioral traits across clinical subgroups and sensory-cognitive measures.

keywords: brain structural connectome of adhd children
topics.title: ADHD Heterogeneity and Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on ADHD in children and its developmental heterogeneity, including symptom dimensions, subtypes, and comorbidities. It links these clinical patterns to structural and functional brain differences, maturation delay, and longitudinal white matter changes.

keywords: music and emotion in functional connectivity
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Music Processing and Reward
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music is encoded and experienced in the brain, including reward, emotion, memory, and sensorimotor integration. It also highlights links between music engagement, training, and neural specialization across listening and performance.

topics.title: Neural Control of Eating Disorders and Food Choice
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and behavioral mechanisms underlying eating behavior, food choice, and disordered eating, with links to obesity and gastrointestinal-related conditions. It also includes studies of cognitive control and brain correlates measured with fMRI and multimodal imaging.

topics.title: Curiosity, Attention, and Working Memory Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how curiosity and interest relate to attention, working memory, and task engagement, often through neural and behavioral measures. It also includes evidence on uncertainty, prediction errors, and physiological markers such as eye tracking, pupil responses, and skin conductance.

topics.title: Mindfulness and Interoceptive Brain Networks
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine how mindfulness and meditation alter brain networks, interoceptive processing, and emotion regulation. They also link these changes to resting-state coupling, neural activity, and physiological markers such as heart rate variability.

topics.title: Neurobiological markers in schizophrenia spectrum
topics.description: This cluster centers on biomarkers, neuroanatomical variation, and prediction across schizophrenia, psychosis, and related spectrum conditions. It emphasizes heterogeneity, early risk states, and rs fmri-based modeling to understand individual differences in brain structure and function.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Emotion Regulation in Anxiety
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how emotion regulation and cognitive reappraisal relate to anxiety and depressive disorders, with emphasis on amygdala and prefrontal mechanisms. The abstracts highlight neural correlates, functional connectivity, and disorder-specific patterns of emotion processing.

keywords: efficacy of personalized connectivity guide brain stimulation in treatment resistant depression
topics.title: Personalized Neuromodulation for Depression Treatment
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression, especially treatment resistant depression and major depressive disorder, with emphasis on personalized and connectivity-guided neuromodulation approaches. It also includes predictors of response and related stimulation strategies such as rtm, dbs, and electroconvulsive therapy.

keywords: neural correlates of optimal risk taking
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Risky Decision Making
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how the human brain supports risky decision making, reward learning, and value computation, with attention to neural correlates in regions such as the amygdala and striatum. It also includes individual differences and developmental or clinical factors affecting impulse control, fairness, trust, and risk taking.

topics.title: Social Cognition and Brain Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural mechanisms underlying social cognition and social decision making, including perspective taking, social participation, and related behavioral traits. It also emphasizes individualized and network-based approaches such as functional and effective connectivity, predictive modeling, and lesion-based mapping.

keywords: functional and metabolic network signatures of
topics.title: Brain Glucose Metabolism in Diabetes
topics.description: This cluster centers on how glucose regulation and diabetes relate to human brain metabolism, network organization, and structural or functional brain changes. It includes studies of acute glycemic effects, insulin action, and multimodal imaging approaches to link metabolic state with neural function.

topics.title: Hormonal Effects on Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how puberty, adolescence, and menopause-related hormonal changes shape brain structure, connectivity, and cognitive development. It also includes studies of hormone therapy and sex-specific neural responses across the menstrual cycle and midlife transition.

topics.title: Depression Biomarkers and Symptom Associations
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related mood disorders, emphasizing symptom profiles, suicidality, and developmental or sleep-related depressive phenotypes. It also highlights biomarker and brain-structure associations, including metabolomic and proteomic signatures and links to cardiometabolic risk.

topics.title: Depression Network Dysfunction and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and major depressive disorder, emphasizing brain network dysfunction, heterogeneity, and dynamic resting-state changes. It also highlights links between depressive symptoms, rumination, and antidepressant treatment response.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment, Depression, and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurodevelopmental and psychiatric patterns across adolescence and youth, especially depression, psychopathology, and the influence of adversity and family environment. It also emphasizes sex differences and functional brain connectivity as key dimensions of risk and development.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
topics.title: Stroke Recovery Prediction and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on stroke outcomes, especially functional recovery, motor and cognitive recovery, and clinical outcomes after acute stroke. It emphasizes prediction from lesion effects and altered functional connectivity, including diffusion MRI and multimodal clustering approaches.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
development and evaluation of an intervention for adolescents
topics.title: Neural and Intervention Studies in Dyscalculia
topics.description: This cluster centers on dyscalculia and mathematical skill development, especially how interventions and training relate to math gains and performance. The abstracts emphasize neural activation, network reorganization, and longitudinal changes in parietal numerical systems.

topics.title: Traumatic Brain Injury and Network Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on traumatic brain injury across mild, chronic, pediatric, and repetitive exposure contexts. It emphasizes network-level alterations, multimodal neuroimaging, and structural-functional brain changes associated with symptoms and outcomes.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Multilingual Reading and Dyslexia
topics.description: This cluster centers on language processing in multilingual and bilingual speakers, with emphasis on reading, orthographic and phonological processing, and language control networks. It also includes work on neural dynamics, interhemispheric connectivity, and predictors of dyslexia and reading disability.

keywords: meg evidence for distributed encoding of parts of speech
topics.title: Language Lateralization and Distributed Comprehension
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how language functions are organized across the brain, especially lateralization, dominance, and interactions with broader cortical networks. It also emphasizes neural encoding of speech, grammar, and comprehension in naturalistic and complex language tasks.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Sleep, Brain Dynamics, and Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on human sleep and related brain-state dynamics across consciousness, aging, and anesthesia. It also highlights EEG/fMRI measures of connectivity, complexity, and glymphatic/CSF coupling, including links to insomnia and memory impairment.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Tinnitus and Misophonia
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neural basis and correlates of tinnitus and misophonia, including symptom dimensions such as loudness, suppression, and responses to triggering stimuli. Studies examine auditory and network-level activity using EEG, fMRI connectivity, and causal analysis approaches.

keywords: decode voice identity in auditory working memory
semantic information in environmental sound categories
topics.title: Auditory Processing and Semantic Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the auditory cortex and broader auditory networks support speech, voice identity, and the semantic processing of environmental sounds. It also includes work on receptive fields, cortical mapping, and functional gradients in human MEG and related recordings.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Glioma Surgery, Connectivity, and Cognitive Outcome
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how glioma and brain tumor surgery alter brain connectivity and functional network organization, especially in relation to lesion location and preoperative disconnection. It also emphasizes predicting postoperative deficits, functional recovery, cognitive impairment, and survival outcomes.

topics.title: Brain Development in Preterm and Heart Disease
topics.description: This cluster focuses on altered brain structure and connectivity in children and adolescents born preterm or with congenital heart disease. It also links these neurodevelopmental differences to cognitive and behavioral outcomes.

keywords: microstates pipeline for source level connectivity and classification in multiple sclerosis
topics.title: Neural markers of fatigue in multiple sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue in multiple sclerosis, with emphasis on identifying neural correlates such as functional connectivity, EEG microstates, and iron deposition. It also includes multimodal MRI and assessment approaches linking brain networks to cognitive performance and slow processing speed.

topics.title: Naturalistic fMRI Decoding and Prediction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on fMRI-based decoding and prediction of naturalistic brain responses, including cross-subject and zero-transfer settings. It also highlights multimodal and language-model approaches for narrative, speech, and brain-network analyses, with an emphasis on reproducibility.

keywords: collaborative infrastructure around shared tool interfaces for neuroimaging
topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on open-science infrastructure for neuroimaging, with emphasis on standards, data organization, and shared platforms. It highlights tools and workflows for BIDS conversion, cloud-enabled collaboration, visualization, and clinical research support.

topics.title: Personalized Deep Brain Stimulation in Parkinson Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation for parkinson disease, especially subthalamic nucleus targets, clinical outcomes, and factors that predict response. It also emphasizes personalized modeling, connectomic analysis, and preoperative imaging markers for stratifying patients and optimizing programming.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound for Essential Tremor Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor, especially Vim-targeted interventions and their durable clinical outcomes. It emphasizes cerebellothalamic connectivity, longitudinal network changes, and functional mapping or connectivity fingerprints associated with treatment response.

keywords: delineation of squirrel monkey brainnetome atlas
topics.title: White Matter and Subcortical Connectivity Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on tractography-based mapping and segmentation of white matter pathways and subcortical structures across human and primate brains. It emphasizes atlas delineation, microstructural characterization, and identification of key hubs and fiber systems.

keywords: infant laminar cortical myelination associated with behavior and environment
topics.title: Laminar Myelination and Iron Development
topics.description: Studies of how cortical laminar microstructure, myelination, and iron accumulation change across early development and relate to behavior and environment. The cluster emphasizes longitudinal MRI-based characterization of white matter and intracortical maturation patterns.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
cardiac pulsatility generates structured network specific bold dynamics
topics.title: Cerebrovascular and BOLD Neuroimaging Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on cerebrovascular physiology and hemodynamic imaging signals, especially cerebrovascular reactivity, blood flow, and BOLD-related dynamics. It also includes multimodal approaches linking fMRI, ASL, cardiac pulsatility, and cerebrospinal fluid oscillations.

keywords: normative modelling of brain volume for diagnostis and prognostis in multiple sclerosis
classification of white matter lesions in
topics.title: MRI Biomarkers in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based measures of brain structure and lesion evolution in multiple sclerosis, linking atrophy, lesion burden, and other imaging abnormalities to disease severity, disability, and cognition. It also includes normative and clinical workflows for diagnosis and prognosis.

keywords: neurodegenerative outcomes in genetic frontotemporal dementia
topics.title: Microstructural MRI markers of cognitive decline
topics.description: These studies examine diffusion and related MRI markers of white matter and cortical microstructure across aging and neurodegenerative conditions. The cluster emphasizes spatial patterns, longitudinal trajectories, and diagnostic relevance for cognitive decline and memory performance.

keywords: maturation trajectories of functional brain network topology
topics.title: Brain Network Development Across Youth
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain network organization changes from infancy through adolescence, with emphasis on developmental trajectories, functional topography, and individualized network structure. It also includes multimodal approaches linking brain connectivity to cognitive performance in young populations.

keywords: individualized mapping of functional brain networks
topics.title: Functional Brain Aging Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how functional brain organization and connectivity change across lifespan, older adulthood, and healthy cognitive aging. It also includes individualized and precision mapping approaches to relate age-related neural changes to cognitive outcomes.

topics.title: MRI and Biomarkers in Parkinsonian Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on imaging and biomarker signatures of Parkinsonian and prodromal Lewy body disorders, especially substantia nigra degeneration, iron accumulation, and neuromelanin MRI. It also includes longitudinal and progression-oriented studies linking structural and functional measures to motor and executive decline.

keywords: bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
functional upper limb motor representation relation
topics.title: Motor Representation and Action Observation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how action observation, imagery, and grasp-related behaviors shape motor representations and sensorimotor network organization, especially in primary motor cortex and cortico-cerebellar circuits. Several abstracts also address body representation and rehabilitation-related modulation of these networks, including virtual reality mirror therapy after stroke.

topics.title: Dynamic Connectivity in Alzheimer’s Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer’s disease and related cognitive decline, with emphasis on APOE-related risk, MCI, and early disease stages. A major theme is altered default mode network and resting-state dynamics, including dynamic functional connectivity and structural-functional coupling.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
topics.title: Neural and AI Vision Representation Alignment
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how human visual representations, especially in face and ventral occipitotemporal regions, align with deep neural network models. It links fMRI-based decoding and voxel selectivity analyses to representational hierarchy and behavioral performance.

topics.title: AI Biomarkers for Early Alzheimer Detection
topics.description: The cluster centers on AI and machine learning approaches for detecting Alzheimer disease and predicting dementia onset from brain imaging and biomarker data. It includes deep learning, foundation models, and multimodal clinical imaging analyses aimed at identifying structural and functional disease patterns.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
topics.title: Alpha Oscillations and Cognitive Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how alpha-band neural activity and oscillatory dynamics relate to cognition, attention, memory, and subjective mental states. It also includes work on individualized alpha entrainment and EEG/MEG signatures linked to performance and brain-state changes.

topics.title: Brain Age Markers for Dementia Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain age and related imaging markers for detecting cognitive impairment, predicting decline, and improving dementia and Alzheimer diagnosis. It also includes normative modeling and regional brain-age approaches across diverse cohorts for subtype differentiation and biomarker development.

keywords: amyloid vulnerable cortices exhibit higher perfusion
topics.title: Amyloid-Tau Heterogeneity in Alzheimer Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on how amyloid and tau relate to Alzheimer disease progression, heterogeneity, and regional vulnerability, including links to resilience and biomarker trajectories. It also highlights subtype differences and interactions with cerebrovascular and reserve-related factors.

keywords: map sensory integration use compositional geometry
topics.title: Visual Specialization and Hierarchical Sensory Integration
topics.description: This cluster centers on how visual and sensory cortical systems organize information across hierarchical maps, boundaries, and specialized regions. It also links representational geometry and naturalistic fMRI to cross-species comparisons of shared neural codes and perception-action integration.

topics.title: Hippocampal Dynamics in Memory and Attention
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal and neural correlates of memory, attention, and working memory, including load effects, recall, and episodic processing. It also includes allocentric coding and circuit-level mechanisms such as pattern separation and dynamic network organization.

keywords: task derive functional connectivity captures individual differences
topics.title: Individual Differences in Functional Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional MRI captures stable and variable aspects of brain organization across individuals, especially during naturalistic and task-based conditions. It also includes work on reliability, individualized parcellation, and connectivity-based markers of personal brain structure and function.

keywords: lifespan spectral rest state eeg normative model
time vary scale free brain dynamic
topics.title: Harmonized EEG Modeling Across Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on building harmonized, reproducible EEG/MEG pipelines for multi-site data and robust cross-site generalization. It also emphasizes unsupervised modeling, brain-age prediction, and lifespan normative analysis of spectral and scale-free brain dynamics.

topics.title: Diffusion MRI Tractography and Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI methods for reconstructing fiber architecture and estimating tissue microstructure, especially in human and ex vivo brain data. It emphasizes tractography, model optimization, and contrast enhancement for improved characterization of white matter organization.

keywords: cerebellar growth predict domain specific cerebral maturation socio linguistic behaviors
topics.title: Cerebellar and Cortical Development Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental mapping of cerebellar and cortical structure, with emphasis on segmentation, morphology, lamination, and microstructural profiling. It also includes atlas and growth-chart approaches linking cerebellar development to broader neurodevelopmental outcomes.

topics.title: Deep Learning for Synthetic Neuroimaging in Stroke
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning methods for synthesizing MRI contrasts and perfusion maps, especially for stroke imaging without contrast agents. It also includes robust longitudinal segmentation and neuroimaging preprocessing workflows for clinical and translational applications.

keywords: convolutional neural network for brain age prediction
deep learning base brain age prediction
topics.title: Deep Learning for Brain MRI Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning approaches for brain MRI analysis, including brain age prediction, anomaly detection, reconstruction, and harmonization across sites and modalities. It also emphasizes efficiency, data augmentation, and quality control for robust neuroimaging workflows.

keywords: task evoked tonic aperiodic eeg activity
topics.title: Aperiodic and Oscillatory EEG Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on EEG markers of brain dynamics, especially alpha oscillations, aperiodic components, and their links to cognition and attention. It also includes dynamic and network-based approaches for characterizing resting-state and task-evoked brain activity, sometimes in simultaneous EEG-fMRI settings.

topics.title: High-Resolution fMRI Connectivity and Reliability
topics.description: These studies focus on high-resolution fMRI measures of human brain organization, especially connectivity, structure-function coupling, and brain-state modeling. A recurring emphasis is on reliability, test-retest stability, and improving sensitivity for neuroimaging analyses.

topics.title: Cross-Species Brain Atlas Construction and Alignment
topics.description: This cluster centers on building and aligning brain atlases and templates across species using histological and surface-based reconstruction methods. It includes automated parcellation, segmentation, and reference-space mapping for comparative neuroanatomy and aging studies.

keywords: benchmarke spatiotemporal fetal brain atlas construction
topics.title: Fetal and Neonatal Brain Atlas Construction
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping and modeling early brain development, especially fetal cortical folding, gyrification, and neonatal atlas building. It also includes segmentation and pipeline-based methods used to construct and compare developmental brain imaging resources.
